name: Cycloid DevEx Platform API
what_it_does: Developer Experience platform with REST API — enabling platform engineering teams to build internal developer portals with self-service infrastructure catalogs, manage cloud resources via a unified portal, enforce cost management, and provide teams with GitOps-based environment provisioning, combining service catalog, infrastructure automation, and cost visibility.
use_cases: Building self-service infrastructure catalogs via Cycloid API for developer environment provisioning
Managing cloud environments via Cycloid API for GitOps-based infrastructure automation
Tracking cloud costs via Cycloid API for engineering resource optimization and showback
Creating service templates via Cycloid API for standardized infrastructure patterns
Integrating CI/CD pipelines via Cycloid API with Concourse for automated deployments
not_for: Simple service catalog without infrastructure automation (use Cortex or OpsLevel)
Teams without cloud infrastructure (Cycloid is infrastructure-automation focused)
Small teams without dedicated platform engineering function
best_when: A platform engineering team wants a European IDP with strong infrastructure self-service and cost management — combining service catalog with actual cloud provisioning automation.
avoid_when: You need US-hosted data residency, simple scorecards without infrastructure automation, or open-source self-hosted.

auth.notes: JWT-based authentication. API token via Authorization: Bearer header. Token obtained via POST /user/login with email/password (exchange credentials for JWT). OAuth2 available for SSO integration (SAML/OIDC). API at cycloid.io/api/v1/ or self-hosted endpoint. Organization and team context required in API paths. Concourse CI/CD integration built-in. Terraform provider available.

pricing.notes: French company (Paris). Founded 2015 by Benjamin Brial and Romaric Philogène. EU-headquartered IDP. Cycloid differentiator: infrastructure-first IDP — combines service catalog with infrastructure automation (Terraform, Pulumi, Ansible). Cost management: cloud cost visibility and allocation. Stacks: reusable infrastructure templates (like Backstage Software Templates but with actual Terraform). Concourse CI: built-in CI/CD pipeline for infrastructure automation. GitOps-native: all config in Git. Self-hosted option available. GDPR by design (French company). Used by: European enterprises, financial services.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JWT via credential exchange (not standard API key). Organization context in all paths. Concourse CI built-in. Terraform/Pulumi stacks. French company (GDPR). EU data residency. Self-hosted option. SAML/OIDC SSO.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Infrastructure stack creation: use stack canonical (slug) for idempotent management. Environment provisioning: state-based — check current state before creating. JWT: obtain once, reuse until expiry.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT authentication via credential exchange (/user/login) — not a simple static API key
JWT expiry: manage token refresh for long-running automation (typically 24-hour expiry)
Organization slug and team context required in most API paths — capture at auth time
Infrastructure-first IDP: API actions may trigger actual cloud resource provisioning
Concourse CI pipelines run automatically on stack operations — understand side effects
Stacks (infrastructure templates) are the core unit — learn the stack model before API integration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. JWT via credential exchange — token has finite expiry (better than static keys). SAML/OIDC SSO for enterprise IdP integration. GDPR by design (French company). ISO27001 certified. Infrastructure automation API — actions can create/destroy cloud resources (high blast radius). Self-hosted option: operator responsible for security. EU data residency by default. Concourse CI integrated — pipeline credentials must be secured.
